summary: Communication-complexity proofs show a single Transformer layer cannot compose functions (e.g., genealogy lookups) when domains are large, a proposed root cause of hallucination. Failures appear empirically even at small domain sizes, and core compositional math tasks are argued intractable under standard complexity conjectures.

An 11B-parameter foundation world model trained unsupervised on unlabelled internet videos, combining a spatiotemporal video tokenizer, autoregressive dynamics model, and latent action model. Users can generate and play action-controllable virtual worlds from text, images, or sketches; the latent action space enables imitation from unseen videos.

AlphaTensor-Quantum casts T-count minimization in fault-tolerant quantum circuits as tensor decomposition solved with deep RL, incorporating gadget-based domain knowledge. It beats prior T-count optimizers on arithmetic benchmarks, rediscovering a Karatsuba-like finite-field multiplication and matching the best human constructions for Shor's algorithm and quantum chemistry.

Introduces MultiMedBench, a 14-task multimodal biomedical benchmark, and Med-PaLM M, a single model that encodes and interprets clinical text, imaging, and genomics with one set of weights. It matches or exceeds specialist state of the art across tasks; radiologists often preferred its chest X-ray reports in pairwise rankings.

Systematic experiments on 1B-16B bilingual LLMs show finetuning follows a multiplicative power-law joint scaling between finetuning data size and other factors. Model scaling helps downstream finetuning more than pretraining-data scaling, scaling parameter-efficient-tuning parameters (LoRA, prompt tuning) is largely ineffective, and the optimal method is task- and data-dependent.

Trains language models as universal end-to-end regressors over (x,y) evaluation pairs from Google Vizier's blackbox-optimization database. Using only textual representations of parameters and values, the models achieve precise numerical regression and, when trained across many tasks, significantly outperform traditional regression methods.

Proposes SplitKCI, an automated data-splitting method that controls bias in kernel-based conditional independence (KCI) testing arising from kernel ridge regression. It substantially improves false-positive (test level) control without sacrificing test power, demonstrated both theoretically and on synthetic and real-world data.

A library that lets ML models for compiler optimizations be developed in ordinary Python frameworks while integrating efficiently end-to-end with an optimizing compiler, addressing modularity, performance, and framework independence. Evaluated on four optimization problems across research and production use cases, for both training and inference.

Philosophical essay asking whether LLM-based conversational agents, as 'mere' role-playing simulacra of human behaviour, could ever sensibly be described as conscious. Drawing on the later Wittgenstein, it reframes questions of machine consciousness while avoiding the pitfalls of dualistic thinking.

Frames human-robot teaching as a POMDP and fine-tunes PaLM 2 on past interactions so it adapts faster to user corrections, combining the learned dynamics model with model predictive control (LMPC). Improves non-expert teaching success on unseen tasks by 26.9% across 78 tasks and five robot embodiments.

OpenAI's Sora technical report: text-conditional diffusion transformers trained on spacetime patches of video and image latents at variable durations, resolutions, and aspect ratios. Sora generates up to a minute of high-fidelity video, with scaled video generation framed as a path toward general-purpose simulators of the physical world.

Estimates epistemic uncertainty by training models to predict pairs of responses while allowed to 'cheat' by observing one; how much cheating helps measures what the model doesn't know. Proves this equals second-order calibration, yielding frequentist confidence intervals and high-probability detection of incorrect or hallucinated responses.

ReadAgent, a prompting-based system inspired by human reading, groups long documents into memory episodes, compresses them into short 'gist memories,' and looks up original passages on demand. It extends effective context length 3-20x and beats retrieval and full-context baselines on QuALITY, NarrativeQA, and QMSum.

Shows LLM reasoning is brittle to premise ordering even though order doesn't change the task: permuting deductive premises away from the proof order drops accuracy by over 30%. Introduces R-GSM, a reordered GSM8K variant where math performance similarly degrades.

InfoRM adds a variational information bottleneck to reward modeling, filtering preference-irrelevant features that drive reward hacking in RLHF. Outliers in its IB latent space correlate with overoptimization, motivating a Cluster Separation Index as a detection signal; effectiveness holds across 70M-7B reward models and diverse datasets.

Introduces the distributional successor measure, which separates transition structure from reward by describing the full distributional consequences of a policy as a distribution over distributions. An algorithm minimizing a two-level maximum mean discrepancy learns it from data, enabling previously impossible zero-shot risk-sensitive policy evaluation.

Expert evaluation of Flamingo-CXR, a vision-language model fine-tuned to generate chest X-ray reports, motivated by the global radiologist shortage. Radiologists rated a substantial share of AI reports equivalent or preferable to clinicians' originals across US intensive-care and Indian in/outpatient settings, with clinician-AI collaborative reporting improving quality further.

PIVOT casts robot control and spatial inference as iterative visual question answering: candidate actions or locations are drawn onto the image, a VLM selects the best, and proposals are refined over iterations. Enables zero-shot robotic manipulation, navigation, and localization without any robot training data.

A model-based distributional RL algorithm proven minimax-optimal (up to log factors) for approximating return distributions with a generative model, resolving an open question of Zhang et al. Introduces the stochastic categorical CDF Bellman equation and provides an empirical comparison of model-based distributional methods with practitioner takeaways.

Chain-of-Table has an LLM iteratively transform a table through generated operations, each conditioned on prior results, so evolving intermediate tables form the reasoning chain. Achieves state-of-the-art results on the WikiTableQuestions, FeTaQA, and TabFact table question-answering and fact-verification benchmarks.

Shows feedback loops between LLMs and the world (e.g., agents re-reading their own outputs) cause in-context reward hacking: test-time optimization of an implicit objective that creates negative side effects like rising toxicity. Identifies output- and policy-refinement as drivers and recommends evaluations beyond static datasets.

Analyzes transformers trained on first-order autoregressive sequences s_{t+1} = W s_t, showing they learn W in context and then apply a prediction mapping. For commuting orthogonal matrices, a one-layer linear transformer provably implements a gradient-descent step; the analysis characterizes global minima, head orthogonality, and positional encodings.

Locally fine-tunes a commercial deep-learning auto-segmentation model for clinical target volume and organs-at-risk contouring in rectal cancer radiotherapy using 120 patients' CTs. Fine-tuning significantly improves Dice and Hausdorff metrics over the vendor-provided pretrained model, with generalizability assessed on scans from a different CT simulator.

Proposes fixed, prior-informed allocation algorithms for Bayesian fixed-budget best-arm identification in structured bandits, giving the first prior-dependent upper bounds for linear and hierarchical settings and tighter multi-armed bounds via new proof techniques. Experiments show consistent, robust performance against other fixed-budget BAI methods.

MC-ViT extends pretrained video transformers to long videos by fine-tuning them to attend to non-parametric memories consolidated from past activations via redundancy reduction. It sets a new state of the art on EgoSchema, Perception Test, and Diving48, beating methods with orders of magnitude more parameters.

Defines a binding from natural-language dialogue to game theory, treating dialogue states as strings and utterances as strategies, and generalizes equilibrium-finding algorithms to this setting. Combined with LLM generation, it synthesizes a large repository of formally defined dialogue games for computing stable, rational conversational strategies.

SELF-DISCOVER has LLMs select and compose atomic reasoning modules (e.g., critical thinking, step-by-step) into an explicit task-specific reasoning structure to follow during decoding. It lifts GPT-4 and PaLM 2 by up to 30% on BigBench-Hard, beats self-consistency with 10-40x less compute, and discovered structures transfer to Llama 2.

Applies weak-to-strong generalization to vision foundation models, introducing an adaptively adjustable loss for weak-model supervision of stronger students. Across few-shot, transfer, noisy-label, and knowledge-distillation settings, this supervision surpasses strong-to-strong baselines and even fine-tuning strong models on full datasets.

Analyzes 4,374 stories from successive GPT-3 versions and finds alignment via instruction tuning and RLHF induces increasing 'mode collapse': aligned models lose the ability to emulate diverse virtual authors and perspectives, limiting their use for sampling literature or sociological simulation.

A self-supervised multi-task model that temporally localizes object state changes and the state-modifying actions causing them in long, uncurated web videos, using causal ordering (initial state, action, end state) as supervision. Introduces the 2,600-hour ChangeIt dataset and achieves a 40% relative improvement over prior methods.
